---
name: Physics critique / red-team report
about: You think a model assumption, derivation, or number is wrong. Finding a fatal flaw here is a headline contribution, not an attack.
title: "Critique: <topic>"
labels: red-team
---

<!-- The manuscripts state which assumptions are load-bearing
     (Part I §2, Part II §§2-3, 8). Every claim in the papers and
     notes/ is labeled demonstrated / in-model / open — please quote
     the claim you're challenging with its label. -->

**The claim being challenged**
<!-- File/section and the exact statement. -->

**Why it's wrong (or doubtful)**
<!-- Your argument, derivation, or reference. Equations welcome.
     "This assumption is unjustified" is a valid critique — say why. -->

**What would settle it**
<!-- A run, a derivation, a paper, an experiment. If you can state a
     falsification condition, we'll pre-register it in notes/. -->

**Severity, in your view**
<!-- Fatal to the design / changes a headline number / bookkeeping. -->
